title: The Conspiracy and Mad Occultists
wikitext:
[]: The Conspiracy and Mad Occultists             Quote Post by gchristnsn » Sun Jul 29, 2012 3:11 am I have understood why you are so skeptical about the subject, you still see Seele as benefactors of the humanity, but I already do not see them in a such way, and the problem is in that I don't see what you see. Because it's quite reasonable to say that a person who see things which hardly anyone saw is mad, I'll try to explain a little bit how we understand things and how invisible becomes visible. I hope, that it will help to show that my claims are reasonable and why there is no shame to not see things for so long. Our brain builds the model of the reality in the form of networks of images (which are in turn represented as networks of encoded visual, audio and other data). The model may be used to describe reality, for example, to answer questions about non-obvious things, to predict things and so on. Networks of images may form objects, networks of objects may form complex objects and so on. Let's call a tightly interconnected network of objects a context (we get them when we learn something). There are some essential things related to the process of understanding, such as analysis, synthesis and pattern matching. We may do analysis or synthesis by pattern matching against a context; we analyze when we check structure of a known object against a context, we may synthesise when we search an unknown object in a context by its structure. So, our ability to understand defined at least by the amount of contexts we have and by the pattern matching abilities of our brain (which are work in unconsciousness). So, if you will get as much facts as possible, connect them as you can and memorize this network, you will get a rough model you could then maintain and develop using astonishing pattern-matching abilities of human brain. When you have a relatively precise model you can non-contradictory answer all reasonable questions concerning facts related to a modeled object, so this why I asked you to answer questions - your model may have parts that absent in my one, questions may force you to rebuild your model. As I found, most of people which I tried to interact do not have a reasonable model, but there is absolutely no shame in this, because the subject of modeling is very complex and only systematic approach allows to adequately model it. Moreover, in the case of NGE you need to use synthesis at first to get the overall picture and then analysis to check it (in such cases you generally use analysis). The complementation conspiracy is the thing which could be obtained only by synthesis; for example, presence of a truth suggest that there should be a lie and presence of a triple agent suggest that there should be at least two information barriers, so there should be a thing which resembles a conspiracy. Then you could use analysis to analyze its structure (the following description is copied from the thread about the third impact). SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: The Conspiracy and Mad Occultists             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sun Jul 29, 2012 10:55 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Jul 29, 2012 1:15 pm Who in the hell ever thought that SEELE were the benefactors of humanity? They want to kill everyone.

[]: Re: The Conspiracy and Mad Occultists             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jul 29, 2012 7:12 pm gchristnsn wrote: I have understood why you are so skeptical about the subject, you still see Seele as benefactors of the humanity, but I already do not see them in a such way, and the problem is in that I don't see what you see.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jul 30, 2012 12:52 am AuraTwilight wrote: Who in the hell ever thought that SEELE were the benefactors of humanity?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Jul 30, 2012 1:31 am Mr. Tines wrote: SEELE themselves.

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Mon Jul 30, 2012 2:45 am I know there are some post-human wankers who say SEELE had the right idea bouncing around /tg/ and /a/. Not many others in meatspace and none in-setting, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jul 30, 2012 5:44 am Der Kommissar wrote: I know there are some post-human wankers who say SEELE had the right idea bouncing around /tg/ and /a/. Not many others in meatspace and none in-setting, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Mon Jul 30, 2012 9:10 am Monk Ed wrote: Incorrect on a technicality: Things which don't exist can't think anything.

[]:             Quote Post by Seoul Gamer » Mon Jul 30, 2012 10:52 am gchristnsn, I have absolutely no idea what it is you're trying to say about Evangelion. Perhaps English isn't your first language, but can you please just state the points you are trying to make? Preferably without a long, confusingly-worded and intractable explanation? I'd love to join in with this discussion, but I haven't a clue what it is you're actually arguing about, much less your reasoning. The most I've picked up is that there's some kind of "conspiracy" and that you believe that your theory is the only logical explanation to some of the show's plot holes. Honestly, the actual logic behind why anything happens in Evangelion is pretty shaky. You can talk all day about the model you've built up, but why should everyone else just assume your model is the most logical one? Why is this even important to discussing the themes and relevance of Evangelion?
